solutions.solution: (d) From Statements I and II, morning walk is good = na pa ta sa wish you good morning = ta na sa da ∴ walk = pa/ta So, both the statements are not sufficient to answer the question.
(d) From Statements I and II, do not go = ka ma tok go to market = mo jo ta ∴ market = jo/ta So, both the statements are not sufficient to answer the question.
(d) From Statements I and II, all is ba = very good boy is na pa = she is good ∴ very = ba/na So, both the statements are not sufficient to answer the question.

(e) Given that, a) at hop = we play chess ...(A) From Statement I, ld play = we are honest From Statement II, gob ots a = they play cricket From Statement I and (A), we = hop From Statement II and (A), play = a Clearly, chess = ed So, both statements are required to answer the question.

(b) Given that, 1 4 g = adopt good habits ...(A) From Statement I, 4 7 3 = like good pictures From Statement II, 8 7 g = assign good names habits From Statement II and [A], 6 = habits So, Statement II alone is required to answer the question.
(c) From Statement I, must far = jo la la did you see = jo ta ka Clearly, must = la From Statement II, si you did that = jo si so So, Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question.
(e) From Statement I, she is fat = he ra ca From Statement II, fat boy = ra ke Clearly, fat = ra So, both statements are required to answer the question.
(d) From Statements I and II, call me back = 5 3 1 you can call me any time = 9 4 1 6 3 ∴ call = 3/1 So, both the statements are not sufficient to answer the question.

(c) Given that, over and above = ja na ta ...(A) From Statement I, come and go = ha la ja From Statement II, he and you = da ja ma Clearly, either from (A) and I or from (A) and II, and = ja So, either Statement I or Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question.
(e) From Statements I and II, 5 s 7 3 = flowers are really good 7 s 3 5 = good flowers are available ∴ 5 = really So, both statements are required to answer the question.
(c) The calendar method is helpful in either case. So, either Statement I or II alone are sufficient to answer the question.
(e) According to Sonu, birthday = Thursday/Friday/Saturday According to Raj, birthday = Wednesday/Thursday. ∴ Required day of birthday = Thursday So, both statements are required.

(e) According to brother, birthday = Thursday/Friday/Saturday According to sister, birthday = Thursday Clearly, required day of birthday = Thursday So, both statements are required to answer the question.

(e) According to the Manoj, Required day = 13th June/14th June/15th June According to the Ashok, Required day = 15th June/16th June/17th June ∴ Required day = 15th June So, both statements are required to answer question.

(c) From Statement I, Between Tuesday and Thursday Required day = Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday From Statement II, Required day = Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday 3rd day of week So, either Statement I or II alone is sufficient to answer the question.

(d) Since, M is not available in Statement I while F is not available in Statement II. So, none of the statements alone is sufficient. From Statements I and II, the final diagram is as under M is the nephew or niece of F. Clearly, M is nephew/niece of F but as gender of M is not known, hence definite relation of M in respect of F cannot be determined. So, both the statements are not sufficient to answer the question.
(c) From Statement I, Nalini is the daughter of Sameer. Clearly, Nalini is the daughter of Sameer. So, Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question.
